classObjective: Recognize biased survey questions and identify whose interests they may serve.
workedExamples: 9-3, Example 3 — Calculate and Choose Measures of Center (Selecting Appropriate Measures with Outliers)
warmUp: Is 'Don't you agree that pizza is the best food?' a well-worded survey question?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: leading questions; contextual bias; selecting appropriate measures with outliers; identifying who benefits from biased questions
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice recognizing biased questions and selecting appropriate measures
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying biased survey language and selecting appropriate measures of center
assessment: Exit ticket: identify the bias in a survey question and explain who it might benefit
capReflection: Why might someone phrase a survey question in a biased way, and how does this affect data collection?
